topic: Definition of Quadratics and Standard Form

questions.question: What is the standard form of a quadratic function?
The ancient Babylonians used quadratic equations to calculate the area of a triangle. Which of the following is a quadratic equation?
The quadratic function $f(x) = $ax^2 + bx + c$ has a leading coefficient of 2. What is the value of $a$?
The ancient Greeks used quadratic equations to model the trajectory of projectiles. Which of the following is a key feature of a quadratic function?
The quadratic function $f(x) = $x^2 - 4x + 4$ can be rewritten in the form $f(x) = $(x - $h$)^2 + $k$. What is the value of $h$?
The ancient Chinese used quadratic equations to calculate the area of a rectangle. Which of the following is an example of a quadratic equation?
The quadratic function $f(x) = $x^2 + 2x + 1$ has a vertex at $(h, k)$. What is the value of $k$?
The ancient Egyptians used quadratic equations to calculate the volume of a pyramid. Which of the following is a quadratic equation?
Describe the key features of the quadratic function $f(x) = $x^2 + 2x - 3$. Be sure to include the vertex, x-intercepts, and y-intercept.
Write a quadratic equation in standard form that has a vertex at $(1, -2)$ and an x-intercept at $(2, 0)$. Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Provide examples of real-world applications of quadratic equations
Encourage students to use graphing calculators to visualize quadratic functions
Emphasize the importance of factoring and solving quadratic equations
